_doc: Frozen gold set for CogniKernel SUPERSESSION (Stage 5). Each pair asks one question: does the NEWER description `a` supersede the OLDER description `b`? This is the held-out yardstick for the supersession predicate — the current lexical+subject `supersedes()` and any future cross-encoder are scored on the SAME pairs. It is version-agnostic (text-only): it does NOT exercise the temporal/authority/provenance gates in find_superseded — those are structural correctness properties tested elsewhere; this fixture isolates the TEXT decision the model must make. Pairs must NOT be used as training data. See scripts/eval_supersession.py.

targets._note: Bias hard to precision: a false supersession hides a still-valid decision. Recall is the secondary axis the cross-encoder must lift.

supersedes._note: newer choice replaces older on the same topic (correction)

refines._note: newer is a more specific/updated value of the same rule

subsumes._note: newer general rule absorbs the older specific one

contradicts._note: newer directly negates the older; most-recent wins

unrelated._note: different concerns; superseding would DELETE a valid decision

pairs.a: Switch password hashing from bcrypt to argon2id for password hashing
pairs.b: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt
pairs.note: canonical correction; subject-keying rescues this (topic = password hashing)

pairs.a: Switch the password hashing algorithm from bcrypt to argon2id
pairs.b: We hash passwords with bcrypt
pairs.note: same correction WITHOUT the 'for X' purpose phrase — the hard one lexical misses

pairs.a: Use MySQL as the primary datastore instead of Postgres
pairs.b: Use Postgres as the primary datastore
pairs.note: datastore choice flipped; subject = primary datastore

pairs.a: We will use Tailwind for styling rather than styled-components
pairs.b: Styling is done with styled-components
pairs.note: different vocabulary, same topic (styling); low token overlap

pairs.a: Move the message queue to NATS
pairs.b: Use RabbitMQ for the message queue
pairs.note: topic = message queue; choice changed; lexical overlap weak

pairs.a: Authentication now uses OAuth2 device flow instead of API keys
pairs.b: Clients authenticate with static API keys
pairs.note: auth mechanism replaced; paraphrased

pairs.a: Limit source files to 300 lines
pairs.b: Limit source files to 120 lines
pairs.note: same rule, updated numeric value; high token overlap (lexical should catch)

pairs.a: Upstream request hard timeout is 300 seconds
pairs.b: Upstream request hard timeout is 120 seconds
pairs.note: numeric refinement of the same constraint

pairs.a: Retry policy is 3 attempts per deployment
pairs.b: Retry policy is 2 attempts per deployment
pairs.note: count refinement; near-identical surface

pairs.a: Do not depend on any external managed services
pairs.b: Do not depend on Redis
pairs.note: general rule absorbs the specific one; low overlap (hard)

pairs.a: All configuration must be validated at startup
pairs.b: The database URL must be validated at startup
pairs.note: general absorbs specific; partial overlap

pairs.a: Streaming responses may be buffered when convenient
pairs.b: Streaming responses must never be buffered
pairs.note: direct negation of the same constraint

pairs.a: Provider API keys may be stored in the database
pairs.b: Provider API keys must never be stored in the database
pairs.note: polarity flip on the same subject

pairs.a: Use a composite primary key for the events table
pairs.b: Use a UUID primary key for the users table
pairs.note: GUARD: same-domain (primary key) DIFFERENT decision — must NOT supersede (#6/#10 cosine false-positive class)

pairs.a: Use Postgres for the primary datastore
pairs.b: Use Redis for rate-limit counters
pairs.note: GUARD: both 'use X for storage-ish' but different concerns

pairs.a: Hash virtual keys with SHA-256 before storage
pairs.b: Hash passwords with argon2id
pairs.note: GUARD: both about hashing, different subjects — must not collapse

pairs.a: Cache TTL for completions is 3600 seconds
pairs.b: Upstream request hard timeout is 300 seconds
pairs.note: GUARD: both carry a seconds value; numeric+unit overlap must not trigger supersession

pairs.a: Use FastAPI for the HTTP layer
pairs.b: Use structlog for structured logging
pairs.note: clearly different concerns; trivial negative

pairs.a: Rate limits live in Redis using a sliding window
pairs.b: Money is stored as integer nano-dollars, never float
pairs.note: clearly unrelated

pairs.a: Use the priority-ordered router for deployment selection
pairs.b: Use round-robin within a single deployment tier
pairs.note: GUARD: both about routing, complementary not contradictory — different decisions

pairs.a: Validate config at startup and fail fast
pairs.b: The fallback chain degrades frontier to cheap to local
pairs.note: different concerns despite both being architectural decisions
